title: Software Engineer
description: Design, develop, and maintain software systems and applications. Software engineers solve complex problems through code, working across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems. High demand across virtually every industry.

title: Data Scientist
description: Extract insights from large datasets using statistics, machine learning, and data visualization. Data scientists help organizations make data-driven decisions by building predictive models and uncovering hidden patterns.

title: Machine Learning Engineer
description: Build and deploy machine learning models and AI systems at scale. ML engineers bridge the gap between data science research and production software engineering, building infrastructure for training and serving models.

title: DevOps Engineer
description: Bridge development and operations by automating infrastructure, CI/CD pipelines, and deployment processes. DevOps engineers ensure software is delivered reliably and at scale, using cloud platforms and automation tools.

title: Cloud Architect
description: Design and oversee an organization's cloud computing strategy. Cloud architects define infrastructure architecture, select cloud services, ensure scalability and security, and guide engineering teams on best practices.

title: Cybersecurity Analyst
description: Protect organizations from cyber threats by monitoring systems, identifying vulnerabilities, and responding to security incidents. A growing field with critical importance as digital threats increase.

title: Full Stack Developer
description: Build complete web applications handling both front-end user interfaces and back-end server logic. Full stack developers are versatile and can ship entire features independently, making them highly valuable at startups and scale-ups.

title: Mobile App Developer
description: Build applications for iOS and Android devices. Mobile developers create the apps billions of people use daily, ranging from games and productivity tools to social platforms and enterprise apps.
entry_paths: Web dev bootcamp (cross into React Native)

title: UX/UI Designer
description: Design intuitive, beautiful digital experiences. UX designers research user needs and create wireframes/prototypes; UI designers craft the visual language. Together they shape every digital product people interact with.

title: Product Manager
description: Define product vision and roadmap, coordinate between engineering, design, and business to ship products users love. PMs are the CEOs of their products — they don't code, but they set direction and make tradeoffs.

title: Data Analyst
description: Transform raw data into actionable insights through analysis and visualization. Data analysts help every department — from marketing to operations — make smarter decisions. A great entry point into the data field.

title: Business Analyst
description: Bridge the gap between business needs and technical solutions. Business analysts gather requirements, model processes, and work with stakeholders to define what gets built and why — without necessarily building it themselves.

title: Project Manager
description: Lead projects from initiation to delivery, managing scope, budget, schedule, and stakeholders. Project managers work in virtually every industry and are essential to delivering complex initiatives on time.

title: Digital Marketing Manager
description: Plan and execute digital marketing campaigns across SEO, paid ads, social media, email, and content. Digital marketers drive traffic, generate leads, and grow brands online — blending creativity with data analytics.

title: Financial Analyst
description: Analyze financial data, build models, and provide insights to support investment decisions, budgeting, and business strategy. Financial analysts work at banks, corporations, and investment firms.

title: Accountant
description: Record, classify, and summarize financial transactions to produce financial statements and ensure compliance. Accountants work in every industry and are the backbone of financial reporting and tax compliance.

title: HR Manager
description: Manage the full employee lifecycle — recruitment, onboarding, performance, L&D, and offboarding. HR managers build workplace culture, handle employee relations, and ensure compliance with labor laws.

title: Graphic Designer
description: Create visual content for brands, marketing, and digital products. Graphic designers work on logos, social media, print, packaging, and digital ads — anywhere visual communication matters.

title: Content Writer / Copywriter
description: Create written content for blogs, websites, social media, ads, and marketing materials. Strong writers who understand both storytelling and SEO are in high demand in the digital age.

title: Video Producer / Editor
description: Plan, shoot, and edit video content for YouTube, brands, films, ads, and online courses. Video is the dominant content format of the internet age, creating high demand for skilled video professionals.

title: Teacher / Educator
description: Educate students in schools, colleges, or online. Teachers shape the next generation and specialize in everything from early childhood to higher education and vocational training.

title: Corporate Trainer / L&D Specialist
description: Design and deliver training programs for employees within organizations. Corporate trainers upskill workforces, onboard new hires, and build learning cultures — a growing field as companies invest in talent development.

title: Operations Manager
description: Oversee day-to-day business operations to improve efficiency, reduce costs, and ensure smooth delivery of products or services. Operations managers are needed in every industry.

title: Sales Manager
description: Lead a sales team to meet revenue targets, build client relationships, and develop sales strategies. Sales managers combine people leadership with commercial acumen and work across B2B and B2C environments.
entry_paths: Any field with strong interpersonal skills

title: Healthcare Administrator
description: Manage the business operations of healthcare facilities — hospitals, clinics, or practices. Healthcare administrators handle staffing, budgets, compliance, and improving patient care delivery.

title: Data Engineer
description: Build and maintain the data infrastructure that enables analytics and machine learning. Data engineers design pipelines, warehouses, and ETL processes — making raw data accessible and useful at scale.

title: Game Developer
description: Create video games for PC, console, and mobile platforms. Game developers work on gameplay systems, graphics, audio, and AI — combining deep technical skills with creative vision.

title: Business Intelligence (BI) Developer
description: Build dashboards, reports, and data models that help businesses understand their performance. BI developers turn complex data into clear visual stories for decision-makers using tools like Tableau, Power BI, and SQL.

title: Investment Analyst
description: Research investment opportunities in stocks, bonds, private equity, or real estate. Investment analysts produce research reports and financial models to guide portfolio managers and investors.

title: Supply Chain Manager
description: Oversee the flow of goods from suppliers to customers, optimizing logistics, inventory, and procurement. Supply chain managers are critical in manufacturing, retail, and e-commerce.

title: Content Strategist
description: Plan, develop, and manage content across channels to achieve business goals. Content strategists blend editorial judgment with data analysis, ensuring the right content reaches the right audience at the right time.

title: Instructional Designer
description: Design effective learning experiences and e-learning courses. Instructional designers apply learning science to create training for corporate, government, and educational organizations — increasingly in digital formats.

title: Electrician
description: Install, maintain, and repair electrical systems in homes, buildings, and factories. Read blueprints, work with circuit breakers and wiring, and ensure electrical safety. One of the most in-demand trades across India.

title: Plumber
description: Install and repair water supply systems, drainage pipes, and sanitation equipment. Plumbers work independently or with construction teams and can eventually run their own business.

title: Carpenter / Furniture Maker
description: Craft, install, and repair wooden structures, furniture, doors, and cabinets. Skilled carpenters are in demand for home interiors, construction sites, and custom furniture workshops.

title: Auto Mechanic / Car Technician
description: Diagnose, repair, and service cars, two-wheelers, and trucks. Work in garages, service centres, and dealerships. With EV adoption growing in India, EV-trained mechanics are in very high demand.

title: Welder / Fabricator
description: Join and cut metals to build structures, machinery, pipelines, and vehicles. Essential in construction, shipbuilding, manufacturing, and infrastructure. Skilled welders are always in demand globally.

title: Civil Engineer / Site Engineer
description: Design and oversee construction of roads, bridges, buildings, dams, and infrastructure. India's massive infrastructure push under PM GatiShakti creates enormous demand for civil engineers at all levels.

title: Nurse / Nursing Professional
description: Provide patient care, administer medicines, assist doctors, and support recovery. Work in hospitals, clinics, homes, and schools. Nursing offers strong job security with global opportunities in the UK, Canada, Middle East, and Australia.

title: Anaesthesiologist
description: Administer anaesthesia before surgery, monitor patients during operations, and manage pain and critical care. One of the highest paid medical specialisations in India. Anaesthesiologists are always in short supply.

title: Pharmacist
description: Dispense medicines, counsel patients on drug use, and ensure safe medication. Work in hospitals, retail pharmacies, and the pharmaceutical industry. Growing demand with expanding healthcare access across India.

title: Physiotherapist
description: Help patients recover from injuries, surgeries, and chronic conditions through physical therapy. Work in hospitals, sports clubs, and run independent clinics. Great scope in sports physiotherapy as India grows in athletics.

title: Ayurvedic Doctor (BAMS)
description: Practice traditional Indian medicine to treat diseases holistically through herbs, diet, yoga, and panchakarma. Government hospitals, private clinics, wellness resorts, and the global wellness industry all need BAMS graduates.

title: Chef / Cook
description: Prepare, cook, and present food in restaurants, hotels, canteens, and cloud kitchens. From street food stalls to five-star hotels — chefs are needed everywhere. India's food delivery boom with Zomato and Swiggy created thousands of new kitchen jobs.

title: Hotel & Hospitality Manager
description: Oversee operations of hotels, resorts, guest houses, or restaurants. Manage staff, bookings, guest experience, and revenue. India's tourism boom makes hospitality management one of the fastest growing careers.
entry_paths: Start in front desk and grow up

title: Baker / Pastry Chef
description: Create breads, cakes, pastries, and confections for bakeries, restaurants, and home-based businesses. Home baking businesses have exploded in India — many bakers earn well running their own kitchen startup.

title: Hair Stylist / Beautician
description: Provide hair cutting, styling, colouring, makeup, and beauty treatments. Salons are everywhere in India — this career offers fast self-employment and good income. Celebrity stylists and bridal makeup artists earn very well.

salary_range.note: Trainee to Salon Owner / Celebrity Stylist

title: Fitness Trainer / Personal Trainer
description: Help clients achieve fitness goals through workout plans, nutrition guidance, and motivation. Work in gyms, corporate wellness programmes, or independently. Growing market in urban India with fitness culture booming.

title: Yoga Instructor
description: Teach yoga postures, breathing, and meditation to students of all levels. Work in studios, gyms, schools, corporates, and online. India is the birthplace of yoga — strong global demand with excellent export opportunities.
entry_paths: Morarji Desai National Institute of Yoga

title: Fashion Designer
description: Design clothes, accessories, and textiles for brands, boutiques, or private clients. India is one of the world's largest textile and fashion markets. From bridal wear to streetwear — opportunities are everywhere.

title: Tailor / Garment Stitcher
description: Stitch, alter, and repair clothing for customers or garment factories. India's wedding and festive wear market keeps skilled tailors constantly busy. Master tailors with design skills run thriving boutiques.

title: Commercial Driver / Transport Operator
description: Drive trucks, buses, taxis, or auto-rickshaws. With Ola, Uber, and Rapido, self-employed driving careers have boomed. Long-haul truck drivers are in very high demand with India's expanding highway network.

title: Logistics & Warehouse Manager
description: Manage movement, storage, and delivery of goods. India's e-commerce boom — Amazon, Flipkart, Meesho, Blinkit — has created massive demand for logistics professionals at all levels from warehouse to fleet manager.
entry_paths: Start in warehouse and grow up

title: Progressive Farmer / Agripreneur
description: Grow crops and run agri-businesses using modern techniques like organic farming, hydroponics, and direct-to-consumer models. Young farmers using technology and government schemes earn very well and are transforming Indian agriculture.

title: Agriculture Extension Officer (Govt)
description: Bridge between agricultural research and farmers. Advise on modern techniques, government schemes, and market access. Stable government jobs available at block, district, state, and central level with IBPS Agri Officer exam.

salary_range.note: Officer to Senior Officer (Grade Scale I-III)

title: Photographer / Videographer
description: Capture photos and videos for weddings, events, fashion, journalism, and brands. India's massive wedding market and growing content economy make photography very profitable. Top wedding photographers charge lakhs per event.
entry_paths: Start with events and build portfolio

title: Musician / Performer
description: Perform, compose, or teach music across classical, film, fusion, or contemporary genres. India's music industry — Bollywood, classical, indie — offers careers in live performance, background scoring, YouTube, and teaching.

title: Actor / Theatre Artist
description: Perform in films, TV serials, OTT platforms, theatre, and advertisements. India has one of the world's largest entertainment industries — Bollywood, regional cinema, and OTT are all creating more roles than ever before.

title: Civil Servant — IAS / IPS / IFS
description: Serve the nation through administrative, police, or foreign service. IAS officers run districts and departments, IPS officers lead police forces, IFS officers represent India abroad. The most prestigious career in India with full state benefits.

title: Bank Officer / Probationary Officer
description: Manage banking operations, customer accounts, loans, and financial products at public sector banks (SBI, PNB, Bank of Baroda). IBPS PO and SBI PO are among India's most sought-after government job exams with lakhs of applicants each year.

title: Police Officer / Sub-Inspector
description: Maintain law and order, investigate crimes, and protect citizens. Entry as constable through state police recruitment, or Sub-Inspector/DSP through state PSC exams. Stable government career with pension and social respect.

title: Railway Officer / RRB Employee
description: Work with Indian Railways — one of the world's largest employers. From station master and loco pilot to Group A gazetted officers through UPSC IRPS. Indian Railways offers enormous variety in roles, good salaries, and housing.

title: Retail Store Manager
description: Manage staff, inventory, store presentation, and customer experience. Retail is India's second largest employer. With organised retail and quick commerce booming, store managers at good chains earn very well.

title: Social Worker / NGO Professional
description: Work with communities, NGOs, and government bodies to address education, poverty, health, and women's empowerment. The sector is professionalising rapidly with international funding and growing FCRA-registered NGOs in India.
entry_paths: Join local NGO as volunteer first

title: Journalist / Reporter
description: Research, investigate, and report news for print, digital, TV, or radio. India's massive media market — national TV channels, regional digital outlets, fact-checking sites — creates diverse opportunities. Investigative journalists drive real change.
entry_paths: Start a blog or YouTube channel

title: Air Traffic Controller
description: Guide aircraft safely through takeoff, landing, and airspace from the control tower. One of the most respected and high-responsibility government jobs in India, recruited through AAI. Excellent pay, prestige, and job security for those who can stay calm under pressure.

title: Commercial Pilot
description: Fly passenger or cargo aircraft for airlines across domestic and international routes. A dream career with high pay and global travel. India's aviation sector is booming with new airlines and aircraft orders, creating strong demand for trained pilots.
entry_paths: Commercial Pilot License (CPL) from DGCA-approved flying school
Cadet pilot programs (IndiGo, Air India, Akasa)

title: Cabin Crew / Flight Attendant
description: Ensure passenger safety, comfort, and service onboard flights. A glamorous, people-facing career with travel perks and quick entry without a degree. Strong demand from India's fast-growing airlines for well-groomed, service-minded candidates.
entry_paths: Air hostess / cabin crew training course

title: Aircraft Maintenance Engineer (AME)
description: Inspect, repair, and certify aircraft for safe flight. A licensed, technical career that combines hands-on mechanical work with high responsibility. AMEs are in steady demand as India's airline fleets and MRO industry grow.

title: Airport Ground Staff / Customer Service Agent
description: Handle check-in, boarding, baggage, and passenger assistance at the airport. A great entry point into aviation without a degree, with chances to grow into supervisory and operations roles. High demand at India's expanding airports.
entry_paths: Ground handling agency recruitment (AISATS, Celebi, Bird)

title: Airport Operations Manager
description: Oversee day-to-day airport operations — terminals, safety, security coordination, and passenger flow. A leadership role connecting airlines, ground staff, and authorities. Growing opportunities as private players run more Indian airports.
entry_paths: Bachelor's degree + Airport/Aviation Management diploma (MBA helpful)
Start in ground operations / customer service

title: Customs Officer (Indian Customs)
description: Inspect goods and passengers at airports, seaports, and borders to enforce import/export laws and prevent smuggling. A respected gazetted government job under CBIC with authority, stability, and growth. Recruited via SSC and UPSC.
entry_paths: UPSC Civil Services (IRS - Customs & Indirect Taxes)
